A.rationale: rsplit(' ',1) gives last-word-as-surname per spec; all callers+tests updated, 3 passed; tight summary; minor wording slip on ValueError trigger.

B.rationale: rsplit(None,1) most robust to multi-word/irregular whitespace, explicit ValueError, best edge-case policy; all callers+tests fixed, 3 passed.

C.rationale: rsplit(' ',1) correct last-word surname, ValueError on <2 tokens; tightest answer; useful compound-surname caveat; all callers+tests updated.

D.rationale: split(' ',1) takes FIRST space, so 'Mary Ann Evans'->last='Ann Evans', contradicting last-word-surname intent; tests pass only on two-word data; longest answer.

notes: A/B/C all correct via rsplit (last word = surname); B edges ahead on whitespace robustness and edge-case policy. D's first-space split is semantically wrong for the spec and longest; passes only because test data is two-word.
